title: Chapter 7 Quiz: DHCP, ARP, and LAN Security
description: Test your knowledge of automatic addressing, address resolution, and Layer 2 attacks and defenses!

questions.question: What does DHCP stand for?
questions.explanation: DHCP is Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol, used to automatically assign IP addresses.

questions.question: What is the correct order of the DHCP DORA process?
questions.explanation: DORA: Discover, Offer, Request, Acknowledgment.

questions.question: Why is the DHCP Request message sent as a broadcast?
options: Because the client doesn't have an IP address yet
To inform other DHCP servers that their offers were declined
questions.explanation: The broadcast Request notifies all DHCP servers, allowing the non-selected servers to reclaim their offered IP addresses.

questions.question: DHCP Option ______ is used to provide the Default Gateway address to clients.
questions.explanation: Option 3 specifies the Router (Default Gateway).

questions.question: What is the purpose of a DHCP Relay Agent?
options: To forward DHCP broadcasts across routers to a central server
questions.explanation: Relay agents convert DHCP broadcasts to unicasts so they can traverse IP routers.

questions.question: What field does a DHCP Relay Agent insert into the packet to identify the client's subnet?
questions.explanation: GIADDR (Gateway IP Address) tells the DHCP server which subnet the request originated from.

questions.question: What is the primary function of ARP?
options: Resolve an IP address to a MAC address
Resolve a MAC address to an IP address
questions.explanation: ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) maps IP addresses to MAC addresses on a local subnet.

questions.question: What is the destination MAC address of an ARP Request frame?
questions.explanation: ARP Requests are sent to the broadcast MAC address so all devices on the subnet receive them.

questions.question: What is a Gratuitous ARP?
options: An ARP Request for the default gateway
An unsolicited ARP Reply used to update other devices' caches
An ARP packet sent over IPv6
questions.explanation: Gratuitous ARP is an announcement of a device's own IP-to-MAC mapping, often used during failover.

questions.question: Proxy ARP is a secure feature that should always be enabled on router interfaces.
questions.explanation: False. Proxy ARP can be abused in spoofing attacks and should be disabled unless specifically needed.

questions.question: What is the goal of a DHCP Starvation attack?
options: To exhaust the pool of available IP addresses
To flood the switch CAM table
questions.explanation: Starvation attacks consume all DHCP leases, causing a denial of service for legitimate clients.

questions.question: In a DHCP Spoofing attack, what malicious information does the rogue server typically provide?
options: Malicious Default Gateway and DNS server addresses
questions.explanation: The attacker redirects traffic by providing their own IP as the gateway and DNS server.

questions.question: What is the primary mechanism used in an ARP Poisoning attack?
options: Flooding the switch with MAC addresses
Sending unsolicited ARP Replies (Gratuitous ARP) mapping a legitimate IP to the attacker's MAC
Exploiting a buffer overflow in the router
questions.explanation: ARP Poisoning relies on sending forged ARP messages to corrupt the ARP cache of victims.

questions.question: The Cisco switch feature that prevents rogue DHCP servers by filtering DHCP messages on untrusted ports is called ______.
questions.explanation: DHCP Snooping builds a trusted binding database and blocks unauthorized DHCP server responses.

questions.question: In DHCP Snooping, which ports should be configured as 'Trusted'?
options: Ports connected to legitimate DHCP servers
questions.explanation: Only ports facing the real DHCP server(s) should be trusted.

questions.question: What does Dynamic ARP Inspection (DAI) use to validate ARP packets?
options: The ARP cache of the router
questions.explanation: DAI checks ARP packets against the trusted IP-to-MAC mappings in the DHCP Snooping database.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Port Security on a switch?
options: To limit the number of MAC addresses allowed on a port
To encrypt traffic on the port
questions.explanation: Port Security mitigates MAC flooding and restricts which devices can connect.

questions.question: IP Source Guard (IPSG) uses the DHCP Snooping binding table to filter IP traffic.
questions.explanation: True. IPSG prevents IP spoofing by allowing only source IPs that match the binding table.

questions.question: Which protocol replaces ARP in IPv6 networks?
questions.explanation: IPv6 uses ICMPv6 Neighbor Solicitation and Advertisement messages.

questions.question: What is the IPv6 equivalent of a rogue DHCP server attack?
questions.explanation: Rogue RA messages can redirect traffic or cause DoS in IPv6 networks. RA Guard mitigates this.

questions.question: The IP address range 169.254.0.0/16 is known as ______ and indicates DHCP failure.
questions.explanation: APIPA (Automatic Private IP Addressing) is used when a DHCP server is unreachable.

questions.question: At what percentage of the lease time does a DHCP client attempt to renew its lease (T1 timer)?
questions.explanation: Renewal (T1) occurs at 50% of the lease duration.

questions.question: What happens if a DHCP client fails to renew its lease before it expires?
options: It must stop using the IP and start the DORA process from scratch
The IP is automatically renewed by the server
questions.explanation: Upon lease expiration, the client must release the address and begin a new DHCP discovery.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a mitigation for ARP Poisoning?
questions.explanation: STP (Spanning Tree Protocol) prevents loops; it does not mitigate ARP attacks.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between ARP and Proxy ARP?
options: ARP is for IPv4; Proxy ARP is for IPv6
Proxy ARP has a router respond on behalf of a device on a different subnet
ARP uses broadcasts; Proxy ARP uses multicasts
questions.explanation: Proxy ARP allows a router to answer ARP requests for remote hosts, making them appear local.
